---
title: "CI ranking helpers"
output: html_document
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r development, include=FALSE}
library(testthat)
```

<!--
 Source of truth for one fusen feature area. Run the inflate chunk at the bottom after editing.
-->

```{r development-load}
# Load already included functions if relevant
pkgload::load_all(export_all = TRUE)
```

# Weighted ranks and concentration-index scoring

```{r development-rank_wt}
# You can prepare the code of the rank_wt() function here
```
  
```{r function-rank_wt}
#' Compute weighted fractional ranks
#'
#' Compute midpoint fractional ranks after sorting `x` in ascending order and
#' normalizing positive weights to sum to one.
#'
#' Observations with missing `x`, missing `wt`, or non-positive `wt` are
#' removed before ranking. The resulting ranks are useful as a low-level
#' building block for concentration-index calculations and related inequality
#' measures.
#'
#' @param x A numeric vector to rank.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of case weights with the same length as `x`.
#'
#' @return A numeric vector of weighted fractional ranks in `(0, 1)`, one for
#'   each retained observation. The output is ordered like the retained inputs
#'   after removing cases with missing `x`, missing `wt`, or `wt <= 0`.
#'
#' @export
rank_wt <- function(x, wt) {
  stopifnot(length(x) == length(wt))
  ok <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(wt) & wt > 0
  x <- x[ok]
  wt <- wt[ok]

  if (!length(x)) {
    return(numeric(0))
  }

  o <- order(x)
  x <- x[o]
  wt <- wt[o] / sum(wt)

  r <- numeric(length(x))
  if (length(x) == 1L) {
    r[1L] <- 0.5
  } else {
    r <- c(0, cumsum(wt[-length(wt)])) + wt / 2
  }

  out <- numeric(sum(ok))
  out[o] <- r
  out
}
```
  
```{r example-rank_wt}
rank_wt(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 1, 2))
rank_wt(c(1, 2, NA, 3), c(1, 0, 1, 2))
```
  
```{r tests-rank_wt}
test_that("rank_wt computes weighted midpoint ranks", {
  expect_equal(
    rank_wt(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 1, 2)),
    c(0.125, 0.375, 0.75)
  )
})

test_that("rank_wt returns ranks in the order of retained inputs", {
  expect_equal(
    rank_wt(c(20, 10, 30), c(1, 1, 2)),
    c(0.375, 0.125, 0.75)
  )
})

test_that("rank_wt drops missing and non-positive weights before ranking", {
  expect_equal(
    rank_wt(c(2, NA, 1, 3), c(1, 1, 0, 2)),
    c(1 / 6, 2 / 3)
  )
})

test_that("rank_wt handles edge cases", {
  expect_equal(rank_wt(5, 2), 0.5)
  expect_equal(rank_wt(c(1, 2), c(0, NA)), numeric(0))
  expect_error(rank_wt(1:2, 1))
})
```

```{r, function-ci_factory}
#' Fast concentration-index score for internal repeated scoring
#'
#' @description
#' Computes the same concentration-index variants as [ci_factory()], but avoids
#' constructing temporary data frames and avoids [stats::cov.wt()]. The weighted
#' covariance uses the same unbiased convention as `stats::cov.wt()` with
#' normalized weights.
#'
#' @param y A two-column numeric matrix with rank variable and outcome.
#' @param wt Non-negative case weights.
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, or `"CIc"`.
#'
#' @return A single non-negative concentration-index score.
#'
#' @noRd
.ci_fast_score <- function(y, wt, type) {
  ok <- stats::complete.cases(y) & !is.na(wt) & wt > 0
  if (!any(ok) || sum(ok) <= 1L) {
    return(0)
  }

  rank <- y[ok, 1]
  outcome <- y[ok, 2]
  wt <- wt[ok]

  total_wt <- sum(wt)
  if (!is.finite(total_wt) || total_wt <= 0) {
    return(0)
  }

  n <- length(wt)
  ord <- order(rank)
  wt_norm <- wt / total_wt
  wt_ord <- wt_norm[ord]

  rank_ord <- numeric(n)
  if (n == 1L) {
    rank_ord[1L] <- 0.5
  } else {
    rank_ord <- c(0, cumsum(wt_ord[-n])) + wt_ord / 2
  }

  rank_w <- numeric(n)
  rank_w[ord] <- rank_ord

  mean_rank <- sum(wt_norm * rank_w)
  mean_outcome <- sum(wt_norm * outcome)
  cov_denom <- 1 - sum(wt_norm^2)

  if (!is.finite(cov_denom) || cov_denom <= 0) {
    return(0)
  }

  cov12 <- sum(wt_norm * (rank_w - mean_rank) *
    (outcome - mean_outcome)) / cov_denom

  if (type == "CI") {
    if (!is.finite(mean_outcome) ||
        abs(mean_outcome) <= .Machine$double.eps) {
      return(0)
    }
    return(abs(2 * cov12 / mean_outcome))
  }

  if (type == "CIg") {
    return(abs(2 * cov12))
  }

  rng <- max(outcome, na.rm = TRUE) - min(outcome, na.rm = TRUE)
  if (!is.finite(rng) || rng <= .Machine$double.eps) {
    return(0)
  }

  4 * abs(2 * cov12) / rng
}

#' Create concentration-index scoring functions
#'
#' Build a function that computes a weighted concentration-index criterion from
#' a two-column response matrix. The first column is treated as the
#' socioeconomic ranking variable and the second as the health outcome.
#'
#' This factory is intended for inequality-aware split scoring, where different
#' concentration-index variants can be swapped in while keeping a common
#' `function(y, wt)` interface.
#'
#' @param type One of `"CI"`, `"CIg"`, or `"CIc"` selecting the standard,
#'   generalized, or corrected concentration index.
#'
#' @return A function with signature `function(y, wt)` where `y` is a two-column
#'   numeric matrix and `wt` is a vector of positive case weights. The returned
#'   function drops incomplete rows and non-positive weights, then returns a
#'   single non-negative concentration-index value. It returns `0` when fewer
#'   than two valid observations remain or when the required denominator is
#'   degenerate.
#'
#' @details The returned function first converts `y[, 1]` to weighted
#'   fractional ranks using [rank_wt()]. It then computes the weighted
#'   covariance between rank and outcome and maps it to the requested index:
#'   `"CI"` divides by the weighted mean of the outcome, `"CIg"` returns the
#'   generalized form, and `"CIc"` applies an Erreygers-style range correction.
#'   Absolute values are returned.
#'
#'   In mathematical notation, for weighted fractional rank `R`, outcome `Y`,
#'   weighted outcome mean `mu`, and weighted covariance `cov_w`, the three
#'   implemented scores are:
#'   \deqn{CI = \left|2 cov_w(R, Y) / \mu\right|}
#'   \deqn{CIg = \left|2 cov_w(R, Y)\right|}
#'   \deqn{CIc = 4\left|2 cov_w(R, Y)\right| / (max(Y) - min(Y))}
#'
#'   These scores are used as non-negative within-node inequality impurities in
#'   the greedy tree and forest. A value closer to zero means less
#'   socioeconomic-related inequality in the outcome within that node.
#'
#' @references
#' Wagstaff A, van Doorslaer E, Watanabe N (2003). "On Decomposing the Causes
#' of Health Sector Inequalities with an Application to Malnutrition
#' Inequalities in Vietnam." *Journal of Econometrics*, 112(1), 207-223.
#' doi:10.1016/S0304-4076(02)00161-6.
#'
#' Wagstaff A (2011). "The concentration index of a binary outcome revisited."
#' *Health Economics*, 20(10), 1155-1160. doi:10.1002/hec.1752.
#'
#' Erreygers G (2009). "Correcting the concentration index." *Journal of Health
#' Economics*, 28(2), 504-515. doi:10.1016/j.jhealeco.2008.02.003.
#' @export 

ci_factory <- function(type = c("CI", "CIg", "CIc")) {
  type <- match.arg(type)

  function(y, wt) {
    stopifnot(is.matrix(y), ncol(y) == 2L, length(wt) == nrow(y))
    .ci_fast_score(y = y, wt = wt, type = type)
  }
}
```

```{r, example-ci_factory)}
ci <- ci_factory("CI")
y <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
ci(y, c(1, 1, 2))
cig <- ci_factory("CIg")
cig(y, c(1, 1, 2))
cic <- ci_factory("CIc")
cic(y, c(1, 1, 2))
```

```{r, tests-ci_factory}
test_that("ci_factory returns scoring functions and validates type", {
  expect_true(is.function(ci_factory("CI")))
  expect_true(is.function(ci_factory("CIg")))
  expect_true(is.function(ci_factory("CIc")))
  expect_error(ci_factory("bad"))
})

test_that("ci_factory computes expected CI variants on a simple example", {
  y <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
  wt <- c(1, 1, 2)

  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  cig <- ci_factory("CIg")
  cic <- ci_factory("CIc")

  expect_equal(ci(y, wt), 2 / 15)
  expect_equal(cig(y, wt), 0.1)
  expect_equal(cic(y, wt), 0.4)
})

test_that("ci_factory matches cov.wt reference calculations", {
  y <- cbind(
    rank = c(4, 1, 3, 2, 5),
    outcome = c(0, 1, 3, 2, 5)
  )
  wt <- c(1, 2, 1, 3, 2)

  reference_score <- function(y, wt, type) {
    ok <- stats::complete.cases(y) & !is.na(wt) & wt > 0
    y <- y[ok, , drop = FALSE]
    wt <- wt[ok]
    y[, 1] <- rank_wt(y[, 1], wt)
    cov12 <- stats::cov.wt(
      x = as.matrix(data.frame(y1 = y[, 1], y2 = y[, 2])),
      wt = wt
    )$cov[1, 2]

    if (type == "CI") {
      return(abs(2 * cov12 / stats::weighted.mean(y[, 2], wt)))
    }
    if (type == "CIg") {
      return(abs(2 * cov12))
    }
    4 * abs(2 * cov12) / (max(y[, 2]) - min(y[, 2]))
  }

  expect_equal(ci_factory("CI")(y, wt), reference_score(y, wt, "CI"))
  expect_equal(ci_factory("CIg")(y, wt), reference_score(y, wt, "CIg"))
  expect_equal(ci_factory("CIc")(y, wt), reference_score(y, wt, "CIc"))
})

test_that("ci_factory drops incomplete and non-positive-weight rows", {
  y1 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30, NA), c(1, 0, 1, 1))
  wt1 <- c(1, 1, 2, 1)

  y2 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
  wt2 <- c(1, 1, 2)

  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  expect_equal(ci(y1, wt1), ci(y2, wt2))

  y3 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30, 40), c(1, 0, 1, 1))
  wt3 <- c(1, 1, 2, 0)
  expect_equal(ci(y3, wt3), ci(y2, wt2))
})

test_that("ci_factory returns zero for insufficient valid data", {
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  y <- cbind(c(10, NA), c(1, 0))
  wt <- c(1, 1)

  expect_equal(ci(y, wt), 0)
})

test_that("ci_factory handles degenerate denominators", {
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  cic <- ci_factory("CIc")

  y_mu0 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(0, 0, 0))
  expect_equal(ci(y_mu0, c(1, 1, 1)), 0)

  y_rng0 <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(5, 5, 5))
  expect_equal(cic(y_rng0, c(1, 1, 1)), 0)
})

test_that("ci_factory is invariant to rescaling weights", {
  y <- cbind(c(10, 20, 30), c(1, 0, 1))
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")

  expect_equal(ci(y, c(1, 1, 2)), ci(y, c(10, 10, 20)))
})
```


```{r, function-weighted_ci_gain}
#' Compute weighted concentration-index gain from a cached parent impurity
#'
#' @description
#' Internal helper used by split-search routines after the parent node
#' concentration index has already been computed. This avoids recomputing the
#' parent impurity for every candidate split in the same node.
#'
#' @param y A matrix with two columns: SES rank variable and health outcome.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of weights.
#' @param left A logical vector indicating the left split.
#' @param ci_fun A function to compute the concentration index.
#' @param ci_parent Precomputed concentration index for the parent node.
#'
#' @return The weighted concentration-index gain.
#'
#' @noRd
.weighted_ci_gain_from_parent <- function(y, wt, left, ci_fun, ci_parent) {
  stopifnot(length(left) == nrow(y), length(wt) == nrow(y))

  wl <- sum(wt[left], na.rm = TRUE)
  wr <- sum(wt[!left], na.rm = TRUE)
  wtot <- wl + wr

  if (wl <= 0 || wr <= 0 || wtot <= 0) return(-Inf)

  ci_left <- ci_fun(y[left,  , drop = FALSE], wt[left])
  ci_right <- ci_fun(y[!left, , drop = FALSE], wt[!left])

  ci_parent - ((wl / wtot) * ci_left + (wr / wtot) * ci_right)
}

#' Compute weighted concentration-index gain for a binary split
#'
#' Compute the reduction in concentration-index impurity obtained by splitting
#' a parent node into left and right child nodes. The gain is the parent node's
#' concentration-index score minus the weighted average of the two child-node
#' scores.
#'
#' @param y A matrix with two columns: SES rank variable and health outcome.
#' @param wt A numeric vector of weights.
#' @param left A logical vector indicating the left split.
#' @param ci_fun A function to compute the concentration index.
#' @return The weighted concentration-index gain.
#'
#' @details For concentration-index impurity `I_m(.)` based on index variant
#'   `m`, parent node `t`, and a candidate split creating left and right child
#'   nodes `t_L` and `t_R`, the gain is
#'   \deqn{G_m(t, j, s) =
#'   I_m(t) - \frac{W_L}{W_t} I_m(t_L) -
#'   \frac{W_R}{W_t} I_m(t_R)}
#'   where `W_t`, `W_L`, and `W_R` are the total case weights in the parent,
#'   left child, and right child. Positive gain means the split reduces the
#'   weighted within-node concentration-index impurity.
#'
#'   In the greedy tree builder, the selected split at node `t` is the
#'   admissible variable-split pair with the largest gain:
#'   \deqn{(j^*, s^*) =
#'   \arg\max_{j \in \mathcal{J}_t,\ s \in \mathcal{S}_{jt}}
#'   G_m(t, j, s).}
#'
#' @references
#' Breiman L, Friedman JH, Olshen RA, Stone CJ (1984). *Classification and
#' Regression Trees*. Wadsworth.
#'
#' Wagstaff A, van Doorslaer E, Watanabe N (2003). "On Decomposing the Causes
#' of Health Sector Inequalities with an Application to Malnutrition
#' Inequalities in Vietnam." *Journal of Econometrics*, 112(1), 207-223.
#' doi:10.1016/S0304-4076(02)00161-6.
#' @export
weighted_ci_gain <- function(y, wt, left, ci_fun) {
  stopifnot(length(left) == nrow(y), length(wt) == nrow(y))

  ci_parent <- ci_fun(y, wt)
  .weighted_ci_gain_from_parent(
    y = y,
    wt = wt,
    left = left,
    ci_fun = ci_fun,
    ci_parent = ci_parent
  )
}
```

```{r, example-weighted_ci_gain}
y <- cbind(
  ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
  health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1)
)
wt <- rep(1, 6)

# A candidate split evaluated outside any tree-fitting routine
left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)

weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, left, ci_factory("CI"))
```

```{r, tests-weighted_ci_gain}
test_that("weighted_ci_gain computes parent minus weighted child impurity", {
  y <- cbind(ses = 1:4, health = c(10, 20, 30, 40))
  wt <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)

  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, left, mock_ci_fun),
    110
  )
})

test_that(".weighted_ci_gain_from_parent matches weighted_ci_gain", {
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50),
    health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1)
  )
  wt <- c(1, 2, 1, 2, 1)
  left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")
  cached_gain <- getFromNamespace(".weighted_ci_gain_from_parent", "ineqTrees")

  expect_equal(
    cached_gain(
      y = y,
      wt = wt,
      left = left,
      ci_fun = ci,
      ci_parent = ci(y, wt)
    ),
    weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, left, ci)
  )
})

test_that("weighted_ci_gain returns -Inf for invalid splits", {
  y <- cbind(ses = 1:4, health = c(10, 20, 30, 40))
  wt <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)

  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(y, wt, c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, TRUE), mock_ci_fun),
    -Inf
  )
  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(
      y, c(0, 2, 3, 4),
      c(TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE), mock_ci_fun
    ),
    -Inf
  )
})

test_that("weighted_ci_gain validates input lengths", {
  y <- cbind(ses = 1:4, health = c(10, 20, 30, 40))
  wt <- c(1, 2, 3, 4)
  mock_ci_fun <- function(y, wt) sum(y[, 2] * wt)

  expect_error(weighted_ci_gain(
    y, wt,
    c(TRUE, FALSE), mock_ci_fun
  ))
  expect_error(weighted_ci_gain(
    y, 1:3,
    c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE, FALSE), mock_ci_fun
  ))
})

test_that("weighted_ci_gain is invariant to global weight rescaling with CI", {
  y <- cbind(
    ses = c(10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60),
    health = c(1, 0, 1, 0, 1, 1)
  )
  left <- c(TRUE, TRUE, TRUE, FALSE, FALSE, FALSE)
  ci <- ci_factory("CI")

  expect_equal(
    weighted_ci_gain(y, rep(1, 6), left, ci),
    weighted_ci_gain(y, rep(10, 6), left, ci)
  )
})
```

```{r development-inflate, eval=FALSE}
# Run but keep eval=FALSE to avoid infinite loop
# Execute in the console directly
fusen::inflate(
  flat_file = "dev/ci_ranking.Rmd",
  vignette_name = "CI ranking helpers"
)
```
